title: cydonia japonica var. genuina
intro: Cydonia japonica var. genuina, also known as the Japanese quince or Japanese chaenomeles, is a deciduous shrub native to East Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral properties. The health benefits of Cydonia japonica var. genuina include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and promote wound healing. Herbal preparations of the plant's flowers, leaves, and roots can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human consumption.
